news.title: Patch v0.1.7
news.body:
Numerous Bug Fixes From Alpha Playtest 1:
Mail fee → 1c, was 50c
Dueling now sets the combat flag, so passive regen using in combat mechanics.
Required_level was never enforced on any cast (Only on learning the spell). Spells should not check level at cast time and error out if you d not meet level requirements.
Autoattack keybind legacy codeRemoved from project.godot, GROUPS, LABELS
Stamina wiringColumn + migration + AbilityEditor field + 10/swing on attack & wand
Chat tabs overhaul, more settings and chat channels can be turns on and off at will.
Loot + crafting chat streams implimented.

news.title: Patch v0.1.6
news.body: Maintenance build. No gameplay changes since v0.1.5 - this is a rebuild from current source with improved build and server-hosting tooling behind the scenes. If you are already on v0.1.5, nothing in game will look or play differently.

news.title: Patch v0.1.5
news.body: Spell scrolls: abilities are now learned from scrolls. The Skill Trainer stocks Scroll: Heal, Scroll: Fireball, and Scroll: Leaping Strike. Reading a scroll permanently teaches you that spell and consumes the scroll. Scrolls are class-restricted - a Warrior can't learn Fireball. Item tooltips now clearly show which ability a scroll teaches and which classes can use it.

news.title: 0.1.1 — First Closed Alpha Release
news.body: The first closed alpha build is live — welcome, Jake! This release ships the full launcher pipeline: auto-update with per-file hash checking, split asset packs so patches only download what changed (usually ~33 MB), and this news feed you're reading right now. Register an account in the launcher, make a character, and go break things — that's the job.

news.title: Welcome to Beacher's Shitty MMO
news.body: A WoW-inspired indie MMORPG built in Godot 4 — manual economy, skill scrolls instead of trainers, and absolutely no auction house. In active development: expect breaking changes, wipes, and rough edges between patches. Feedback welcome.
